feed.title: SAP Community - SAP Master Data Governance
feed.subtitle: SAP Master Data Governance Q&A in SAP Community

entry.title: Hallo, ist es technisch möglich, dass wir aus dem MDG in Ariba springen? Um hier Dokumente zu sehen
entry.content: Ist dies derzeit technisch möglich, dass wir aus dem MDG direkt in Ariba springen können um hier im SLP Dokumente zu lesen oder weiter zu senden?

entry.title: Create CR type dumps internally at CL_FDT_SERVICES_INTERNAL
entry.content:
Scenario: 'Create change Request type and activate 

Standard code dumps at select statement when joining FDT_ADMN_000 , FDT_RSTL_0000 with the version table FDT_ADMN_0030 We could not find the object id causing this dump

Dump :  Any one faced dump DBSQL_TABLE_UNKNOWN CX_SY_OPEN_SQL_DB in the standard class CL_FDT_SERVICES_INTERNAL GET_FUNCTIONS_FOR_RULESETS line 112, please let us know any suggestions regarding this or any latest sap note to solve this.

Environment : S4HANA

Thanks !

entry.title: SAP MDG Multiobject processing Change Request Derivation scenario
entry.content:
Hello Experts ,

Setup

  • MDG 9.2
  • MM Data model
  • Custom Type 1 entity
  • For this custom entity we have created a Multi object processing type change request.

We have a requirement where we need to derive the same values across all the type 1 entities within this multi object processing change request.

I understand that we can not do this using USMD_RULE_SERVICE or USMD_RULE_SERVICE_ET since there we can only perform derivations for  type 1 entity and its type 4 entities but not across multiple type 1 entities.

I understand that there might be a possibility to achieve derivation across multiple type 1 entities using access class. Can someone please help me with an example of how to use access class to achieve such functionality.

entry.title: Error message not preventing users to submit the change request in MDG
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I've a new requirement to add a new field in GENERAL DATA of the change request header. And I've to make this field as mandatory based on some conditions.

Now the condition is working fine, and the error message also showing. But user can continue to submit the change request even though the error message is there. It is not stopping the user.

I've added the code for the validation in GET DATA method of the class - CL_USMD_CR_GUIBB_GENERAL_DATA (Feeder class)

Also I tried to make that as mandatory using fpmgb_s_actionusage, which is also not working as expected. in the UI it is mandatory, but it is also not preventing the user to submit the request.

 

Please correct me if I'm wrong or if any other methods to be followed. 

 

Kind Regards,

Akhil

entry.title: SAP MDG: Description not getting reflecting Change Request Tracker
entry.content:
HI SCN Folks, 

In Change Request Tracker, description not getting reflect


Like what are the ways to get fix....

 

Thanks, 

Suggu Sandeep.

entry.title: Service MDG_REQ_PC is missing in SICF
entry.content:
Hello SCN,

We have activated all necessary business functions however MDG_REQ_PC service is not present while search in SICF. Note : Installed product and software version shown on fiori app documentation are available in system.

Thanks,

entry.title: MRP2 view, in house production time is activated for raw material.
entry.content:
Hi Gurus,

For raw material, even after I've specified that: int. purchase orders are not allowed in OMS2, I still see the inhouse production time in MRP2 view. Please inform how to suppress the inhouse production time for raw material.

 

Regards

Vinay

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG: Description not getting reflecting Change Re...
entry.content:
Hi SCN Folks, 

got the solution the only thing needs to give is - in initiator row, after initiator step member need to assign in Next Step Column, 

Once it get done in SAP MDG IMG, Come back to fiori CR 

 

Thank You,

Suggu Sandeep 🙂

 

SAP Master Data Governance

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG: HANA Search making default for both Customer...
entry.content: It's showing same

entry.title: Queries around Cloud ready mode in sap MDG on S4 HANA 2023
entry.content:
Hello All,

 

Our system is being upgraded to S/4 Hana 2023 on premise. We are planning to implement a Cloud Ready Mode for Business partner (Customer and Supplier). I do have a few queries about it.

 

  1. Currently we are using Ariba SLP for onboarding Suppliers. Is MDG Cloud Ready Mode supports Ariba SLP integration?
  2. How derivation and validation across entity types works in Cloud-ready mode. Do single-entity and across-entity Badis (USMD_RULE_SERVICE) are supported in Cloud-ready mode?
  3. In Automation workflow can system trigger the validation at request submission stage, or a separate step would be required in the workflow? In the current process (Rube base workflow) it does the validation at the Change Request submission stage.
  4. How can we enable a set of fields at each workflow steps (e.g. four level of approvers) in automation workflow?
  5. Can you please provide the product (Material) release date for support in Cloud Ready Mode?
  6. In product/Material Cloud Ready Mode- Is classic classification will be supported for material class (001) in classic mode?

SAP S/4HANA Cloud Master Data   @0012anirban @Mark63  @Rahul15 

 

Thanks

Rahul Khandelwal

entry.title: Mass updates of New Tax Category (condition type_
entry.content:
Dear,

We are working ON S/4 HANA conversion project. now we are doing post conversion activities on S/4 HANA.

when we synchronized customer to BP on ECC, that time TAX category (Tax condition type) was CNXJ. 

now on S/4 we have created new TAX category UTXJ, when we opened BP for existing customer in display mode we don't see this UTXJ in billing tab of sales area (FLCU01) but when we clicked on Change mode, we can see new tax category.

Is this expected behavior of system? what should be do to see see new tax category in display mode only without going to change mode.?

Thank you

entry.title: MDG Consolidation with active records
entry.content:
Hi MDG Experts,
We have created custom template for consolidation with Active records and strategy set as 'Remove duplicates'
After activation step, Best record is updated as per Best record calculation logic and other potential duplicate records are marked for deletion but, Is there any table to get those details like, which records are marked for deletion, which records were selected as selection step, which records were outcome of matching step ?

I am able to check activate record through Manage consolidation tile, but didn't more information through that tile.
As Process is successfully activated now, I am not see data of matching step.

Please guide here with tables/Function module to get those information.

Thanks,
Brijeshkumar

entry.title: Dupicated regions for Mexico
entry.content:
We are implementing SAP in a company, and in the DEV system the regions (states) for the country of Mexico are duplicated.

How can this be fixed, We have not start using it yet.

Thank you.

entry.title: SAP MDG: CR Types of pop-up not shown in NWBC Screen
entry.content:
HI SCN Folks,

Hope you are doing great 🙂

CR Types - Popup not getting displayed in SAP NWBC, Attached Screenshot 

 

Thank You, 

Suggu Sandeep.

SAP Master Data Governance SAP NetWeaver

entry.title: Re: SAP MDG: CR Types of pop-up not shown in NWBC Screen
entry.content:
This is due to not mentioning of Number Ranges in SNRO 

Navigation: SNRO > MDG_BP 

Once It get maintained Intervals, It stated working fine 

Thank You, 

Suggu Sandeep.

SAP Master Data Governance SAP NetWeaver

entry.title: Enabling Custom/Customer Namespace Table Maintenance via MDC for Business Partner
entry.content:
Welcome! to all my fellow MDG consultants. I trust you are in search of content which would help you to implement custom table maintenance via SAP’s MDC framework. Well, your search has ended!!

You have found just the right blog which will hopefully answer all the questions regarding your requirement.

As part of your initial research, I you all might have already scourged the SAP community and knowledge blogs to find the below How to documents.

SAP How-To Guide: Node (Table) Extension for Business Partner in MDG Consolidation and Mass Processing

SAP How-To Guide: Field Extension for Business Partner/Supplier/Customer in MDG Consolidation and Mass Processing

SAP Note 2755336 - CMP: Using Custom Namespaces in Enhancements

If you have not had a chance to go through the above documents, now is a good time to do it!!

For those of you who might have already followed the steps mentioned in the above documents and are still struggling with several errors while enabling real-time maintenance of custom table via MDC, I have decided to easy your pain, by sharing my implementation experiences with you.

The remainder of the blog explains the things that I had to additionally to enable real time custom table maintenance via MDC for process model 147 (Business Partner).

Business Requirement:

Enable maintenance of a table /CEECV/ROFI_BPTY via MDC process model 147 (Business Partner).

Steps To be Followed:

Step 1: Enhance the MDC 147 process model with custom table.

Goto transaction MDCIMG >>Configure Process Models and Field Properties >> Configure Process Models

Add the table /CEECV/ROFI_BPTY in the table lists and select the “Process” check box.

Specify the join with BUT000 table in the process model.

Step 2: Define your custom Data and Model classes.  

Before you generate any process relevant structures for your added custom table it is important that you have defined your own Data and Model classes for processing the 147 (Business Partner) process model.

You can follow the basic instructions mentioned in SAP’s How-To documents (Please refer links provided at the beginning of the blog) for creating and implementing these classes.

The Data Class:

The data class is created by inheriting the standard BP process model’s data class CL_MDC_DATA_BP.

Re-define the constructor of the original class and write the below lines of code.

super->constructor(
      EXPORTING
        io_model = io_model
      iv_table_name = iv_table_name
      iv_process_id = iv_process_id
      iv_step_number = iv_step_number
      iv_step_type = iv_step_type
      iv_package_size = iv_package_size
      iv_process_relevant = iv_process_relevant
      iv_brfplus_allowed = iv_brfplus_allowed
      iv_omit_rules = iv_omit_rules
      iv_skip_own_system = iv_skip_own_system
    ).

IF me->table_name EQ ‘/CEECV/ROFI_BPTY’.

      me->client_field = ‘CLIENT’.                 “MDC process table’s client field
      me->active_client_field = ‘MANDT’.   “Active tables client field

      me->source_id_field = 'BPNUM'.       “Active tables BPnumber field
 ENDIF.

Trust me, the above lines of code given in the IF condition is going to save you from lot of dumps. Had to debug a lot for this!! Even ended up raising an OSS message to SAP for this :).

The Model Class:

The model class is created by inheriting the standard BP process model’s model class CL_MDC_MODEL_BP.

Typically you need to implement the below methods in your custom model class

  1. MAP_EXTENSIONS_2API

Here you write the code to map your custom table data to Business partner API structures.

  1. SAVE_BUSINESS_PARTNER_ACTIVE 

 Here you write the code to save your additional custom table data to DB.

  1. READ_ALL_DATA

Here you write code to read you custom table data.

For all the three methods the implementations can be done as per the business requirement and there are no additional things to be done in the model class.

You can pretty much refer the standard How-To document to implement the model class.

SAP How-To Guide: Node (Table) Extension for Business Partner in MDG Consolidation and Mass Processing

Step 3: Generating the MDC process structures and Tables.

The next step is to generate MDC processing tables and structures for your added custom table, which in our case is /CEECV/ROFI_BPTY.

Please refer the below SAP note for this, as the table is in customer namespace.

SAP Note 2755336 - CMP: Using Custom Namespaces in Enhancements

Also, would like to highlight here that the structure names can be generated as per your requirement.

All that is needed is to redefine the below methods in your custom data class.

It is important that the below code is available and activated before you generate the MDC process structures and tables.

  1. IF_MDC_DATA~ATTRIBUTE_STRUCTURE_NAME

CALL METHOD super->if_mdc_data~attribute_structure_name
      EXPORTING
        iv_name_prefixing = iv_name_prefixing
      RECEIVING
        rv_structure_name = rv_structure_name.

If me->table_name EQ /CEECV/ROFI_BPTY.
        rv_structure_name = '/CEECV/ROBPTY_ATTRIBUTES'.

Endif.

  1. IF_MDC_DATA~KEY_STRUCTURE_NAME

CALL METHOD super->if_mdc_data~key_structure_name
      EXPORTING
        iv_name_prefixing = iv_name_prefixing
      RECEIVING
        rv_structure_name = rv_structure_name.

    If me->table_name EQ ‘/CEECV/ROFI_BPTY’.
        rv_structure_name = '/CEECV/ROBPTY_KEY'.

    Endif.

  1. IF_MDC_DATA~TABLE_NAME_BY_TYPE

rv_table_name = super->if_mdc_data~table_name_by_type( iv_type ).

CASE iv_type.
      WHEN if_mdc_data=>gc_type-source.
        IF me->table_name = ‘/CEECV/ROFI_BPTY’.
          rv_table_name = '/CEECV/RBPTY_SRC'.
        ENDIF.

      WHEN if_mdc_data=>gc_type-process.
        IF me->table_name = ‘/CEECV/ROFI_BPTY’.
          rv_table_name = '/CEECV/RBPTY_PRC'.
        ENDIF.

  1. IF_MDC_DATA~TABLE_TYPE_NAME_BY_TYPE

CALL METHOD super->if_mdc_data~table_type_name_by_type
      EXPORTING
        iv_type            = iv_type
        iv_name_prefixing  = iv_name_prefixing
      RECEIVING
        rv_table_type_name = rv_table_type_name.

CASE iv_type.
      WHEN if_mdc_data=>gc_type-source.
        IF me->table_name = ‘/CEECV/ROFI_BPTY’.
            rv_table_type_name= '/CEECV/TT_ROBPTY_SRC'.
        ENDIF.

      WHEN if_mdc_data=>gc_type-process.
        IF me->table_name = ‘/CEECV/ROFI_BPTY’.
            rv_table_type_name= '/CEECV/TT_ROBPTY_PRC'.
        ENDIF.

The package that will be required to store the generated structures and tables needs to align with the customer namespace in our case /CEECV/. You cannot store a generated customer namespace object in a ‘Z’ or ‘Y ’package.

That's all Folks!! these were the additional things that I did when enabling custom table maintenance in MDC for Business Partner process model. Hopefully, my experience would be helpful for many other aspiring and budding MDG consultants.

Stay tuned for more!!

entry.title: SAP MDG: 500 Error - TYPE NOT FOUND
entry.content:
HI SCN Folks, 

Facing Issue Fiori got stopped, While Creating CR (Customer Governance > Org > Choosing (CR Type)

500 Server Not Found, TYPE Not Found 

 

 

Thank You, 

Suggu Sandeep.

SAP Master Data Governance

entry.title: How to restrict Customer records from being replicated from SAP ECC to ARIBA SLP
entry.content:
Hi Experts,

I am facing an issue when I am trying to replicate only the Business Partners(which are Vendors) from SAP ECC(MDG Co-deployed) to ARIBA SLP via SOA webservice. Here my objective to replicate ONLY the vendor data when they are changed through backend t-codes XK02 or LSMW (emergency change only).

The flow is as follows: I want to replicate the BP(only Vendors, not Customers), when the vendors are changed through the abovementioned backend t-codes.  

SAP ECC --> SOA Service --> CIG --> ARIBA SLP

I have activated the Function Module "MDG_BS_BP_OUTBOUND_DRF" through SPRO configuration as per SAP's recommendation. But, after the said configuration is done, I am observing that, Customers are also being replicated to ARIBA SLP along with the Vendors. In fact, to be precise, it is taking all the changed Business Partners(regardless of Customers or Vendors) from ECC and replicating them to ARIBA SLP. But we do not want any Customer data to be replicated through this replication process. we also have configured the DRF Filter objects only to include Vendors(we have put Vendor Account Groups and BP Roles as Filter parameter) data. But it seems like this replication configuration(Function Module "MDG_BS_BP_OUTBOUND_DRF") is not considering any DRF Filter, as a result of which it is also considering Customers to be replicated. I can see both Vendors and Customers messages in SRT_MONI.

It would be highly helpful if anyone of you kindly advise as to how to achieve it. How to restrict Customer data from being replicated.

Regards, 

T

entry.title: Sending Customers (t-code BD12) to BTP integration suite (SCPI) fails with MDG error
entry.content:
Hi,

Im trying to send customer data via Integration suite to SAP Commerce. 

I have a recent S/4hana trial version. I managed to send MATMAS(products), COND_A (prices) and now im trying to replicate DEBMAS. However when I try to send the data I receive following error:

The current IDoc is related to a customer. Possible IDoc types are:
DEBMAS for customer data
ADRMAS for customer address data
ADR2MAS or ADR3MAS for the customer's contact person address
DOLMAS for the customer's document links

Your system is configured for using SAP Master Governance for Customers. The related business functions and switches are active. This requires DRF Customizing for the client system BTPSCPI. The same is not configured. The IDoc processing cannot continue and is terminated with a short dump.

I tried to follow up with https://me.sap.com/notes/2363365 however I m not able to find all the options described in the note. For example the BTP integration suite is not maintained as Business System not the SAP Commerce as thats a trail.

Is there any option to configure DFR and send DEBMAS data from S/4hana to BTP Integration Suite (former SCPI).
